---
# =============================================================================
# index.qmd — M@TE Home / Landing page
# =============================================================================
title: "M@TE — Model Atlas of the Earth"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<!-- ── Hero ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="mate-hero">
  <h1>M@TE</h1>
  <p class="mate-subtitle">Model Atlas of the Earth</p>
  <p>
    A curated, open atlas of geoscientific numerical models — reproducible,
    reusable, and FAIR.
  </p>
  <br/>
  <a href="models/index.html" class="btn btn-primary" style="background:#D64000; border-color:#D64000; font-size:18px; padding:0.5rem 1.5rem;">
    Browse Models
  </a>
  &nbsp;
  <a href="about.html" class="btn btn-outline-secondary" style="font-size:18px; padding:0.5rem 1.5rem;">
    Learn More
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## What is M@TE?

**M@TE** (Model Atlas of the Earth) is designed to meet geoscientists where they are: from educators
and students using visualizations in the classroom to researchers developing and benchmarking new
numerical models. It lowers the learning curve of numerical models and bridges the gap between
traditional geology and numerical geosciences.

**M@TE enables you to:**

- **Explore and visualize models** through an intuitive web interface: play animations, use them in your teaching or to better understand Earth system concepts.
- **Download and build on models** by accessing input files and configuration scripts to reproduce or expand upon simulations on your own.
- **Analyze and benchmark model outputs** by comparing results across models and datasets without having to recreate simulations from scratch.
- **Upload your own models** using streamlined tools for packaging code, metadata and outputs — stored, preserved and assigned a DOI for citation.

---

## Featured Models

```{=html}
<div class="models-grid">

  <!-- ── Model card: mather-2022-groundwater ─────────────────────────────── -->
  <a href="models/mather-2022-groundwater.html" class="mc-card-container">
    <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ModelAtlasofTheEarth/mather-2022-groundwater/main/.website_material/graphics/fig1.png"
         alt="Groundwater flow model"
         onerror="this.src='https://placehold.co/600x300/D64000/white?text=M%40TE+Model';" />
    <h3>Constraining the response of continental-scale groundwater flow to climate change</h3>
    <p class="mc-card-abstract">
      A highly scalable numerical framework to model continental groundwater flow, capturing
      flow pathways between deep aquifers and the near-surface using a Bayesian framework.
    </p>
    <div class="mc-card-meta">
      <span class="badge-creator">Ben Mather</span>
      <span class="badge-creator">Dietmar Müller</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-tag">groundwater</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">thermal-hydraulic</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">Bayesian</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-doi">
        <span class="badge-doi-left">DOI</span>
        <span class="badge-doi-right">10.25914/t2mn-bk81</span>
      </span>
    </div>
  </a>

  <!-- ── Placeholder card 2 ──────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
  <div class="mc-card-container">
    <img src="https://placehold.co/600x300/2c8ec7/white?text=Subduction+Model"
         alt="Subduction zone model placeholder" />
    <h3>Subduction zone dynamics — a numerical simulation (example)</h3>
    <p class="mc-card-abstract">
      A high-resolution 3D model of subduction zone dynamics exploring the interplay between
      slab geometry, mantle flow, and surface topography across geological timescales.
    </p>
    <div class="mc-card-meta">
      <span class="badge-creator">Jane Doe</span>
      <span class="badge-creator">John Smith</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-tag">subduction</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">tectonics</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">geodynamics</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-doi">
        <span class="badge-doi-left">DOI</span>
        <span class="badge-doi-right">10.xxxxx/placeholder</span>
      </span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- ── Placeholder card 3 ──────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
  <div class="mc-card-container">
    <img src="https://placehold.co/600x300/555/white?text=Mantle+Convection"
         alt="Mantle convection model placeholder" />
    <h3>Mantle convection and thermal evolution of the Earth (example)</h3>
    <p class="mc-card-abstract">
      Global mantle convection model simulating 4.5 billion years of Earth thermal evolution,
      constrained by seismic tomography and geochemical data from ocean island basalts.
    </p>
    <div class="mc-card-meta">
      <span class="badge-creator">Alice Johnson</span>
      <span class="badge-creator">Bob Williams</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-tag">mantle</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">convection</span>
      <span class="badge-tag">thermal evolution</span>
      <br/>
      <span class="badge-doi">
        <span class="badge-doi-left">DOI</span>
        <span class="badge-doi-right">10.xxxxx/placeholder</span>
      </span>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

<div style="text-align:center; margin-top:1.5rem;">
  <a href="models/index.html" class="btn btn-outline-primary" style="border-color:#D64000; color:#D64000;">
    View all models →
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Why M@TE?

```{=html}
<ul class="mate-highlights">
  <li>✅ Open source and free</li>
  <li>🔎 FAIR data principles</li>
  <li>♻️ Reproducible models</li>
  <li>🏗️ Built on sustainable infrastructure</li>
  <li>📦 RO-Crate packaging</li>
  <li>🆔 DOI-assigned datasets</li>
</ul>
```

**Together we build reproducible, reusable, robust models — it takes a village — so please cite the models you use.**
